title: 'Whenever India faced war or ...': PM Modi calls for public responsibility amid Middle East crisis
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi urged collective national efforts to reduce import dependence and strengthen India's global supply chain position. Amidst global uncertainty and Middle East conflict concerns, he appealed to citizens to minimize foreign currency spending and support domestic manufacturing. Modi drew parallels to past national crises, emphasizing historical citizen unity in response to government appeals.

title: 'Has anyone forced you to drink?' CM Abdullah on uproar over liquor shops in J&K
content: Jammu and Kashmir Chief Minister Omar Abdullah defended the presence of liquor shops, stating they cater to individuals whose religions permit alcohol consumption and that no one is coerced. He emphasized his government's policy of not opening new shops and ensuring existing ones were not near youth centers. This stance drew criticism, prompting further clarification from the CM.

title: From Agni-I to Agni-VI: How India's missile mastery keeps Pakistan, China on edge
content: India's recent MIRV-equipped Agni missile test propels its strategic program into global focus. This advancement, a culmination of decades of indigenous development, significantly bolsters India's nuclear deterrence, ensuring survivability and credible retaliation against evolving threats. The Agni series now forms a crucial pillar of India's nuclear triad, reflecting its growing technological prowess and geopolitical ambitions.

title: Michael Burry warns investors: Stocks are not up or down for jobs or consumer sentiment
content: Michael Burry, famed for predicting the 2008 crash, warns the stock market's AI obsession mirrors the dot-com bubble. He believes markets are ignoring economic data, driven solely by momentum. Burry cautions that massive AI investments by tech giants may yield no lasting returns, potentially leading to significant market corrections and tech job stagnation.

title: Virat Kohli once, Priyansh Arya twice! Punjab Kings opener enters IPL record books
content: Priyansh Arya made IPL history by hitting Mitchell Starc for six off the first ball in Dharamsala, marking his third such feat. This places him second behind Yashasvi Jaiswal, who also hit Starc for an opening ball six earlier this season. The match saw Delhi Capitals opt to field after winning the toss.

title: After 43 years, US family sold electrical company for $1.7B, gave workers $240M
content: A US-based businessman, Graham Walker, gifted his 540 employees a staggering $240 million from the $1.7 billion sale of his family's company, Fibrebond Corp. This generous act, where workers received an average of $443,000, stemmed from a condition Walker imposed before the sale to power-management giant Eaton.

title: Suvendu govt to roll out ‘Annapurna Bhandar’ scheme from June 1; women to get Rs 3,000 monthly
content: West Bengal's new BJP government, led by Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari, has launched the "Annapurna Bhandaar" scheme. Eligible women will receive ₹3,000 monthly, replacing the previous "Lakshmir Bhandar." The cabinet also approved integrating the state with central schemes like Ayushman Bharat and addressing illegal immigration.

title: Meta to cut 8,000 jobs and CEO Mark Zuckerberg is making priorities clear for all employees
content: Meta is cutting 8,000 jobs on May 20 as CEO Mark Zuckerberg rebuilds the company around small, AI-powered teams. With capex hitting a record $125–145 billion this year, CFO Susan Li admitted there's no clean answer for what Meta's ideal headcount even looks like anymore. The company wants builders who can ship alone what teams once built together—and is letting everyone else go.

title: Random Musing: Why Keir Starmer is experiencing 'Labour pain'
content: A version of the British Parliament has been around since 1265 in one form or another, and has survived murderous kings, civil and world wars, lord protectors, skirmishes that inspired the Game of Thrones Red Wedding, and one lettuce that briefly became a constitutional actor. It has seen its fair share of entertaining characters.

title: ‘Fifth over for a bowler if ... ': Gavaskar proposes drastic T20 rule change
content: Sunil Gavaskar proposes rewarding bowlers with an extra over for taking three or more wickets in their spell, aiming to rebalance T20 cricket heavily favouring batters. He believes this encourages attacking bowling, citing recent high-scoring IPL matches as evidence of the need for change. Gavaskar suggests testing the idea in domestic tournaments first.

title: Mike Vrabel–Dianna Russini affair rumors resurface after bonding clip
content: Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini might have strongly denied their affair rumors but it has only gotten worse. As Mike Vrabel resumed his professional duties, a recent clip of his with Dianna Russini has sparked outrage among millions as many question the duration of their alleged affair drama between them that is speculated to be brewing for a long time.

title: NFL trade rumor: Bears eye former $17m Commanders star to boost offense
content: The Chicago Bears recorded an impressive run on the field in the last season after an average performance in the 2024 season, sparking a new wave of hope among fans. While the team faced a brutal loss against the Los Angeles Rams in the Divisional Round, all eyes are on the team’s roster for the new season.

title: Naomi Osaka takes hilarious dig at schedule ahead of Swiatek clash
content: Naomi Osaka is ready for another tough battle at the Italian Open 2026. She is preparing to face Iga Swiatek in the Round of 16. The Japanese star booked her place in the fourth round after a dominant 6-1, 6-2 win over Diana Shnaider in Rome. Osaka has shown steady improvement during the clay-court season and now faces one of the strongest players on the surface once again.

title: Celina Jaitly alleges sons moved away amid divorce, claims blocked contact
content: Actress Celina Jaitly revealed on Mother's Day her distress over being unable to contact her sons amid divorce proceedings. She claims her husband, Peter Haag, has moved their children to an undisclosed location and blocked all communication, despite court orders. Jaitly alleges manipulation of Austrian laws to prevent contact and emotional distancing.

title: US passenger evacuated from hantavirus-hit cruise ship tests ‘mildly’ positive
content: According to the department, both passengers travelled in the aircraft’s biocontainment units “out of an abundance of caution.” Officials said the passengers are being transported to a specialised facility in Nebraska, while the individual showing mild symptoms will be shifted to a separate centre. Three passengers aboard the MV Hondius have died, while several others have fallen ill due to the rare rodent-borne disease.

title: 'Kaun Banega Kerala CM?' Suspense lingers as Venugopal, Satheesan camps intensify lobbying
content: Kerala's next chief minister remains undecided as the Congress high command deliberates between K C Venugopal, V D Satheesan, and Ramesh Chennithala. Lobbying intensifies for the top post, with leaders awaiting a final decision based on merit and political considerations. Party president Mallikarjun Kharge and Rahul Gandhi are key in the process, urging unity and discipline.

title: Car AC maintenance guide: Tips to keep cooling at its best
content: Car ACs often fail unexpectedly, but subtle signs usually precede a breakdown. Regular maintenance and smart usage habits are key to keeping your car's cooling system efficient, especially in India's harsh climate. Understanding basic AC functions and following simple tips can prevent costly repairs and ensure comfort all summer long.

title: Dianna Russini scandal: Husband 'caught them again' in Mike Vrabel rumors
content: Fresh speculation surrounding Dianna Russini and Mike Vrabel intensified after journalist John Ziegler suggested Russini’s husband may have known about the alleged affair years ago. The viral theory claims Kevin Goldschmidt had previously confronted Russini and was “wrongly convinced it was over” before newer footage surfaced in 2026. However, no evidence has confirmed who leaked the photos and videos tied to the controversy.

title: ‘The hardest thing about being famous is...': Quote of the day by Al Pacino
content: Veteran actor Al Pacino reveals the unexpected challenge of fame: constant agreement from others. He emphasizes the crucial need for genuine individuals who offer honest feedback, even when it's difficult to hear. Pacino's journey from humble beginnings to cinematic icon underscores the value of truth amidst adulation, urging us to identify such people in our lives.

title: 'We'll make a decision': Mumbai Indians provide big update on Hardik Pandya
content: Mumbai Indians skipper Hardik Pandya is sidelined with a back issue, missing consecutive IPL 2026 matches. Head coach Mahela Jayawardene confirmed the ongoing treatment, stating his return is day-to-day. This setback comes as MI, already eliminated from playoff contention, suffered another defeat, highlighting their disappointing season.

title: 'He wants me to quit wrestling': Vinesh Phogat hits back at WFI president
content: Star wrestler Vinesh Phogat has accused the Wrestling Federation of India (WFI) of conspiring to force her out of the sport. Phogat insists she is eligible to return to competitive wrestling, refuting WFI claims about notice periods and rule violations. The WFI, however, maintains she cannot compete until disciplinary proceedings are concluded.

title: Gaurav Gera defends Ranveer amid vanity van rumours on ‘Dhurandhar’ set
content: Gaurav Gera has dismissed rumors of Ranveer Singh demanding multiple vanity vans on the sets of 'Dhurandhar'. Gera shared a playful anecdote of Ranveer mimicking his 'Chutki' character, highlighting the actor's fun nature. While Farah Khan joked about the vanity van demands, Gera clarified he didn't witness such extravagance.

title: West Bengal govt sacks nominated board members, scraps service extensions beyond age 60
content: In a separate direction, the state government also asked departments to discontinue the re-employment and service extensions granted to officers and officials who continued beyond the usual retirement age of 60 years. "The government has issued an order to take necessary action to immediately terminate the tenures of the nominated members, directors, or chairpersons of different boards, organisations, non-statutory bodies and public sector undertakings of the state government," a senior bureaucrat said.

title: SC asks Mamata to file fresh plea on SIR deletions exceeding Bengal victory margins
content: The SIR of electoral rolls became one of the most fiercely debated issues in the 2026 West Bengal assembly elections, with the exercise significantly reshaping the state’s voter base before polling. Nearly 91 lakh names, around 12 per cent of the electorate, were removed during the revision process. Of these, more than 60 lakh voters were marked as deceased, while nearly 27 lakh cases remained pending or under scrutiny.

title: Why PM Modi wants Indians to avoid buying gold for a year
content: Prime Minister Modi urged Indians to halt gold purchases for a year to protect foreign exchange reserves, a call supported by GTRI which highlighted a surge in gold imports straining the trade balance. Union Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw echoed the appeal, emphasizing reduced import spending amid Middle East conflict, leading to a sell-off in jewellery stocks.

title: 'I will establish my innocence': Vinesh Phogat responds to WFI show-cause notice
content: Wrestler Vinesh Phogat has formally responded to the Wrestling Federation of India's notice. She asserts her eligibility to return to competitive wrestling from January 1, 2026. Phogat claims she has official confirmation from the International Testing Agency. She intends to compete in the ongoing Senior Open Ranking Tournament. Phogat denies anti-doping rule violations and allegations regarding selection trials.

title: Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini controversy revives Vikings Love Boat scandal
content: Fresh online discussion surrounding Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini has revived interest in the Minnesota Vikings’ infamous 2005 “Love Boat” scandal. Fans began comparing the recent boat-related controversy to the wild NFL incident involving reports of sex workers, explicit acts, and nearly 100 women aboard luxury yachts. While the situations differ significantly, the renewed attention has pushed one of football’s most notorious off-field scandals back into public conversation.

title: AIADMK at breaking point? Revolt brews against EPS after Tamil Nadu poll debacle
content: AIADMK faces internal turmoil following a significant electoral defeat in Tamil Nadu, with divisions surfacing over leadership and potential support for the new TVK government. MLAs arrived in separate groups for the assembly, highlighting a visible rift and raising questions about the party's future cohesion and relevance.

title: TVK’s JCD Prabhakar set to become Tamil Nadu speaker unopposed as Vijay backs seasoned hand
content: TVK MLA J C D Prabhakar has filed his nomination for the Speaker of the Tamil Nadu assembly, poised for an unopposed election.  The 73-year-old, a former AIADMK member, brings significant legislative experience to the role, balancing the ruling combine's youthful image.

title: India’s growth story: Q4 FY26 GDP seen at healthy 7.2%, FY27 growth pegged at 6.6%
content: Despite global economic headwinds from Middle East turmoil, India's economy is projected for stable growth through FY26 and FY27, with SBI forecasting around 7.5% for FY26.  Resilient domestic demand, strong credit expansion, and broad-based improvements in high-frequency indicators support this optimistic outlook, though external vulnerabilities like oil prices and currency movements remain key concerns.

title: 'Make every effort to save fuel': Govt reiterates PM Modi's appeal, assures there is no crisis
content: India's government is urging citizens to cut petrol and diesel use. This comes as the Strait of Hormuz situation tightens and global oil prices climb. Prime Minister Narendra Modi has appealed for reduced consumption. Public transport and electric vehicles are encouraged. Fuel supplies remain stable for consumers. However, state-owned oil companies face significant losses.

title: Govt. to bring reforms in AYUSH sector to enhance quality: Health Minister
content: Satya Kumar Yadav says new guidelines and mandatory registration for AYUSH clinics and hospitals will help curb fake drugs and check unqualified practitioners

title: Kanjirappally Diocese to launch golden jubilee celebrations on May 12
content: As part of the Golden Jubilee celebrations, an eparchial assembly will be held at Marian College Kuttikkanam in Idukki from May 12 to 16

title: Allahabad HC blames State government, police for pendency of cases
content: Bench says many young and committed judicial officers are unable to function effectively because of severe staff shortage, lack of police cooperation in executing summons and warrants, and delay in forensic reports

title: Organ racket scam: hospitals likely to come under probe
content: So far, five cases have been registered across the Ernakulam rural and Kochi city police limits, with five persons arrested in connection with the organ donation scam

title: Private bus operators oppose free KSRTC travel for women
content: All Kerala Bus Operators Organisation State general secretary T. Gopinathan terms UDF move unilateral. He says women and students form the majority of passengers who rely on private buses. If women are allowed to travel free on KSRTC services, private buses will be left carrying mostly students

title: Bihar CM Samrat Choudhary gives free hand to police to act tough against criminals
content: The CM says if any criminal dares to challenge the police, the police will respond within 48 hours, and regardless of the caste or religion of the criminals, they will not be spared

title: Labour codes ‘greatest setback’ for workers, says Kharge, slams Centre
content: Mallikarjun Kharge says the government drafted and implemented the “anti-worker” labour codes without consultation and has not convened the Indian Labour Conference since 2015

title: Trinamool alleges continued post-poll violence in West Bengal
content: Abhishek Banerjee alleges statues of tribal leaders Sidhu and Kanhu Murmu have been vandalised in north Bengal

title: Kadapa MLA links Almaspet violence to ganja menace
content: Madhavi Reddy blames anti social elements for taking the situation into their control and allegedly resorting to violence

title: Modi’s austerity drive is an admission of failure on economic and foreign fronts, alleges Akhilesh
content: It appears the BJP government has completely lost control; the dollar is skyrocketing, and the Indian rupee has plummeted, Mr. Yadav said

title: Ensure establishment of multi-speciality hospital in Munnar: Kerala SHRC
content: Commission emphasises that lack of adequate hospital facilities and poor road connectivity are primary reasons for deteriorating health of pregnant women and newborns in district’s tribal settlements

title: Tourism industry urges govt. to boost inbound travel, not just curb overseas trips
content: Responding to PM Modi’s call to defer foreign travel for a year amid the West Asia conflict, tourism bodies say India must focus on attracting more foreign tourists as inbound arrivals remain below pre-pandemic levels while outbound travel continues to surge

title: West Bengal Secretariat moving back to Writers’ Buildings; small traders hope for revival of businesses
content: Owners of tea stalls and small eateries, fruit vendors and traders in the surroundings of the nearly 250-year-old Writers’ Buildings said the return of government employees and visitors is expected to boost their businesses

title: A.P. CM seeks Centre’s intervention in multiple inter-State water disputes, irrigation projects
content: Naidu opposes proposed increase in height of Almatti Dam in Karnataka, seeks speedy approvals for Banakacherla link project and Odisha’s help for land acquisition for Neradi Barrage project

title: Police intensify probe into sexual assault at Kattakada bus station in Kerala, suspect still at large
content: According to the police, the suspect, identified as ‘Bruce Lee’ Shibu, is suspected to have fled to Tamil Nadu

title: Door-to-door Census survey begins across CURE
content: A total of 1.27 lakh households complete online self-enumeration ahead of house listing exercise; a total 19,868 House Listing Blocks were created and digitally demarcated on the portal

title: Odisha to replicate Kerala’s advanced disaster warning infrastructure ‘KaWaCHaM’
content: A delegation from Odisha recently visited Kerala and sought the State’s assistance in establishing a KaWaCHaM-like network

title: Minister lays foundation stone for ₹6 crore expansion works of Kurnool Rythu Bazar
content: The project is expected to benefit nearly 150 farmers and is part of the State government’s plans to develop Kurnool into a smart city with improved civic and market infrastructure

title: Stakeholders dialogue on Kerala’s monsoon preparedness on May 21
content: Titled ‘Tracking the Monsoon’, the event will be hosted in association with the India Meteorological Department, Indian Meteorological Society, Kerala State Disaster Management Authority and Directorate of Environment and Climate Change

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly: Churning in AIADMK becomes evident with split over Leader of Legislature Party post
content: In the morning, a letter, purportedly signed by a group of AIADMK legislators stating that Mr. Palaniswami had been elected the Leader of the Legislature Party, was submitted to the Pro-tem Speaker M.V. Karuppaiah. In the evening, Mr. Shanmugam presented a letter claiming support for the leadership of Mr. Velumani.

title: China may not join alliance on conservation of big cats
content: IBCA, which is being spearheaded by India, is to hold its first-ever summit here from June 1-3, with representatives from 95 countries expected to participate

title: Ongole-breed bulls take centre stage at national-level Bull Strength Competition in Prakasam
content: Thousands gathered at Tellapadu village in Prakasam district to watch the traditional cattle endurance event organised during a temple festival

title: Kerala govt. absolves two forest officials in Aralam LPG distribution case following Vigilance probe
content: Procedural lapses were alleged in a 2017 gas connection distribution scheme for 195 below poverty line tribal households in the Aralam rehabilitation area

title: Telangana BJP distances itself from POCSO case against Bandi Sanjay’s son, calls it legal issue
content: Telangana BJP president dismisses allegations of discrepancies in SIR conducted during the recent Assembly elections

title: Cyber Crime Wing notice for blocking social media posts draws criticism
content: Incidentally, most of the flagged URLs/posts allegedly contained content critical of Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam and Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay

title: Kerala Assembly polls 2026: AICC summons top leaders to Delhi to finalise party’s choice of Chief Minister
content: Those attending the crucial meeting with the party’s high command include former KPCC presidents K. Sudhakaran, Mullappally Ramachandran, V.M. Sudheeran and K. Muraleedharan

title: Religious fervour, enthusiasm mark Huligemma Devi Rathotsava at Huligi
content: The annual chariot festival commenced during the auspicious hours at around 5.30 p.m. at the famous pilgrimage centre in Koppal taluk

title: Suspected explosives on PM Modi’s Bengaluru visit route: NIA joins probe
content: Investigators are examining whether the explosive materials were intentionally placed to target the Prime Minister’s convoy route or whether there is a larger conspiracy behind the incident.

title: 3-year-old girl comes under stray dog attack in her house, hospitalised
content: The dog entered the house through the kitchen door and launched a violent attack on the child. It is believed to have attacked two other local people earlier in the day

title: Annamayya govt. school student emerges runner-up at national Spelling Bee competition
content: Shailesh, studying in Class II of Government Primary School at Veligallu, had earlier secured first place at the district, zonal, and State-level competitions before qualifying for the national event

title: From Banker To PM: How This Tycoon Became America's Pick For Iraq
content: US President Donald Trump has invited Zaidi to Washington and declared that Washington "is with him all the way."

title: Iran War, Oil Sanction, Energy: What Trump And Xi May Focus On During Talks
content: Talks on the Iran war may dominate the May 14 to 15 summit between US President Donald Trump and his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping in Beijing.

title: After Mamata Banerjee's "Unity Call", BJP's "She's Calling Maoists" Charge
content: The BJP said Mamata Banerjee's Trinamool is working to revive Maoists and will bring Maoists in the name of unity

title: WHO Confirms 7 Cases Of Hantavirus On Cruise Ship
content: The tally was updated after France reported that a French passenger evacuated from the MV Hondius had tested positive for the virus.

title: Bhagwant Mann's Cousin Joins BJP In Major Setback To AAP In Punjab
content: Condemning the attacks on BJP offices and workers, state BJP chief Sunil Jakhar warned that "everyone will be held accountable".

title: Harassment, Molestation, Abuse: Women's Panel Findings On TCS Nashik Case
content: The committee found that accused individuals Danish, Tausif and Raza Memon had "assumed effective control" of the TCS Nashik facility, allegedly protected by human resources head Ashwini Chainani.

title: Kanyakumari To Kashmir: Indo-Australian Man Runs To End Leprosy Stigma
content: A childhood memory from Rishikesh, a cricketing hero from Australia, and 4,700 kilometres of road - that is what brought 23-year-old Om Satija back to India. Not as a tourist, but as an ultramarathoner on a mission to end the stigma around leprosy.

title: Opinion: Opinion | 'Hellish Difficulty': Why India Should Be Keeping An Eye On China's New 'Shipborne' Drone
content: The GJ-21, which has a reported combat attack radius of up to 1,500 kilometres, when combined with a land-attack long-range precision-strike system, can have the ability to strike deep within a territory's mainland.

title: Kerala Chief Minister Or Nothing: Sources On VD Satheesan's Big Demand
content: VD Satheesan - who is the choice of the people as well as the Congress's Keral ally and UDF constituent IUML -- is not ready to accept a secondary role.

title: Quest For Rare Bird, Landfill Visit: How 'Patient Zero' Caught Hantavirus
content: The ornithologists decided to visit a landfill four miles outside of Ushuaia, considered a pilgrimage point for birdwatchers, later thought to be the place where they contracted hantavirus.

title: No Barrier, No Traffic Jam: Delhi-NCR's New Toll System Takes Effect
content: The Automatic Number Plate Recognition (ANPR) cameras detect the vehicle registration number and send the information to the FASTag-reading technology installed on the lane.

title: Israel's Iron Dome Intercepted 99% Missiles From Iran, Hamas And Hezbollah
content: Iran has fired about 1,500 ballistic missiles at Israel in two rounds of fighting since 2024, the chairman of Rafael Advanced Defense Systems said.

title: Omar Abdullah Admits His Comment On J&K Liquor Ban Was A "Mistake"
content: On Sunday, Omar Abdullah had refused to respond to a question on banning liquor in Jammu and Kashmir and asked if anyone was being forced to drink.

title: 'Can't Treat Wife Like Animal': Supreme Court Denies Bail In Domestic Violence Case
content: During the hearing, Justice Varale said that there could be no justification for treating a wife "like an animal".

title: US-Iran War News LIVE Updates: Trump Warns Iran Against Moving Uranium, Says US Keeping An Eye
content: US-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: A US admiral said US forces sank six small Iranian ships. Iran denied any had been sunk and earlier fired warning shots at US warships

title: Himanta Biswa Sarma's Big Announcement On Assam's New Council Of Ministers
content: Himanta Biswa Sarma said that the NDA government would continue to work steadily for the welfare and development of Assam.

title: Opinion: Opinion | Anga-Banga-Kalinga And The Making Of A New Eastern Political Corridor
content: Taken together, Anga, Banga, and Kalinga formed a connected eastern system. Anga gave the region its inland Gangetic depth; Banga provided the deltaic and riverine gateway; and Kalinga opened the maritime horizon.

title: UP Bride Refuses Marriage After Groom Falls Ill, Seeks Health Report
content: The incident took place in the Sakit area, where the groom reportedly fell ill during the wedding rituals, triggering a dispute between the two families, police said.

title: "Tareekh Pe Tareekh": Why Allahabad High Court Invoked Sunny Deol's Movie Dialogue
content: Justice Deshwal observed that judicial officers in Uttar Pradesh are frustrated because they are unable to perform their duties due to insufficient staff, non-cooperation by the police, faulty investigations, and improper forensic reports

title: 'Stay calm, avoid panic': Rajnath Singh reviews energy supply amid Middle East crisis
content: govt is taking concrete steps to prevent shortages or disruptions in supply chains, he says . "it is PM’s broader message about conservation during a difficult global period," he adds . poll: do you believe the government is taking adequate steps to ensure energy security?
